Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

(1 comment)

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

it not a good solution. I think we need to return to idea to remove lu_env_item allocation. this avoid first search and will drop this bug completely.

Patch Set 3:

Alexey, please explain what is "first search" and how that can avoid the problem?

Patch Set 3:

I think you should remember a discussion in patch "put all env in list". as I remember, we have agreement to integrate lu_env_item intro lu_env, with this patch no search / free in this point, and no ability to find already freed item.

Patch Set 3:

there must be some confusion. the whole point of the original patch is to be able to search env by current(). what we discussed is to embed this new structure into lu_env to remove allocation, but I don;t see how this can help to solve LU-12491 - lu_env is subject to kfree() as well.

Patch Set 3:

it's correct, it's subject of kfree, but you will remove an ability to find a stale lu_env_item entry, as any rhashtable_lookup_fast will caused an thread which know this lu_env exist and lu_env_item not a last reference for this object.

Patch Set 3:

the root cause is that _alive_ thread finds just released lu_env_item structure. so I don't think embedding can change anything.

Patch Set 3:

if you mean, a different thread want a "NEW" env (via lu_env_find) and found a env in freed state, it looks like a logical bug, as it should be not possible.

Patch Set 3:

sorry, I don't follow.. it's a common list, one thread wants to find own lu_env (by current()), goes over the items. the items are supposed to have valid pointers. one item has just been freed by another exiting thread (potentially reallocated) - so the list is corrupted. 
to avoid this the patch delay kfree() using RCU technique.

Patch Set 3:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

(2 comments)

Unfortunately I found that this doesn't work on my reproducer

Patch Set 3:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 3: -Code-Review

(1 comment)

commit.subject: LU-12491 obdclass: use RCU to release lu_env_item
commit.message:
LU-12491 obdclass: use RCU to release lu_env_item

as rhashtable_lookup_fast() is lockless and can
find just released objects.

Fixes: aa82cc8361 ("obdclass: put all service's env on the list")
